1 /** 2 * Section / SectionHeader — the layout & rhythm primitive every page sprint 3 * (S06–S13) composes. Deliberately STATIC: it owns no GSAP, no ScrollTrigger, 4 * no reveal of its own. Page sprints wrap their content in InkReveal/SplitLines 5 * themselves, so keeping Section animation-free decouples the layout tier from 6 * the motion tier — Section never decides when content reveals. 7 * 8 * Section props: 9 * id string — anchor id forwarded to the root element. 10 * as elem — element/tag to render as (default "section"). 11 * padded bool — when true, vertical section padding via the 12 * --k-section-pad token (default true). 13 * bordered bool — when true, a bottom hairline (border-b border-line). 14 * className string — merged onto the root, after the guaranteed utilities. 15 * children node — section body (placed inside the centered container). 16 * ...rest — spread onto the root element. 17 * 18 * SectionHeader props: 19 * eyebrow string — optional mono micro-label above the title. 20 * title string — the h2 headline (required for a meaningful header). 21 * lead string — optional ink-dim supporting paragraph under the title. 22 * className string — merged onto the header root. 23 * 24 * Gate: NONE. There is no motion to gate; Section renders identically on every 25 * tier. It is the host other primitives reveal *inside*. 26 * 27 * Reduced / mobile fallback: identical to the default render (no motion path). 28 * 29 * Scar notes: 30 * - Scar #4: the root is ALWAYS `relative` so any absolute inset-0 child a 31 * page drops inside is positioned against the Section, not the viewport. 32 * - No injected

1 /** 2 * Lab — dev-only proof bench for the v2 motion primitives (S04). 3 * 4 * Mounts EVERY primitive once with sample content so QA can screenshot and 5 * assert each in isolation. DCE'd out of production: App.jsx only registers 6 * the /lab route under import.meta.env.DEV. 7 * 8 * This page owns NO ScrollTrigger of its own — every trigger/pin belongs to a 9 * primitive. The only Lab-side GSAP is the scrub-scene readout: quickSetters 10 * created in a layout effect inside a gsap.context scoped to the page root and 11 * reverted on unmount. ScrubScene owns the trigger; Lab only consumes progress. 12 * 13 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 14 * PRIMITIVE CATALOG (import path + key props) — required in-file documentation: 15 * 16 * SplitLines "../components/motion/index.js" 17 * {string} 18 * aliases: MaskText, SplitTextReveal 19 * InkReveal {children} 21 * DraftLine 23 * DimensionMark 24 * MagneticButton {…} 25 * MagneticCursor (S05: mounted once globally in RootLayout, not here) 26 * CountStrike 28 * KineticMarquee 30 * ScrubScene {children} 32 * AxisRail {panels} 33 * alias: HorizontalPin 34 * SnapStack {cards} 35 * alias: CardStack 36 * ParallaxLayer {…} 37 * ChapterMarker 38 * Section

# CONTRACTS — the living technical contract

The binding interface between sprints. It grows: most sections below are
skeletons now; the named sprint **appends** to them at runtime. When you add a
selector, id, data-attr, global, or API, you MUST record it here in the same
commit, so QA assertions and later sprints stay in sync.

**Authority:** `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > DIRECTION/TOKENS > sprint file`. This doc
beats DIRECTION and the sprint file on any **locked id / selector / integration
id**. It does not override PROTOCOL.

**How to append:** find your sprint's section, replace its `(empty — …)`
placeholder or add rows beneath the existing ones. Keep the
`<!-- appended by sprint NN -->` marker so the next author knows where their
turn begins. Never delete a prior sprint's entries; if you supersede one, strike
it and add the replacement with a note.

---

## Route map
<!-- appended by sprint 02 (then refined by later page sprints 06-09) -->

The router IA. S02 fills the real rows from `design/DIRECTION.md`'s IA table.
Page sprints (S06–S09) fill in their owned section ids. QA's
`scripts/qa/routes.mjs` mirrors this table — keep them in sync.

| path | component (src path) | owned section ids / anchors | showpiece mount(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `src/pages/Home.jsx` | `#hero #pipeline #industries #services #testimonials #cta` | `data-showpiece="hero"` (CSS/SVG, S10) + `data-showpiece="pipeline"` (WebGL, S11) |
| `/work` | `src/pages/Work.jsx` | `#case-studies #industries #recap` | `data-showpiece="skyline"` (WebGL, S11/S12) |
| `/studio` | `src/pages/Studio.jsx` | `#process #team #manifesto` | — (zero-GL route) |
| `/contact` | `src/pages/Contact.jsx` | `#contact` | — (zero-GL route) |

<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
**S02 router map notes (rewrote the skeleton per DIRECTION §IA):**
- Router: `src/App.jsx` = `<Routes>` with a layout route (`RootLayout.jsx` →
  `StubNav` + `<Outlet/>` + `StubFooter`); catch-all `*` → `NotFound.jsx`
  (unique id `#not-found`). `BrowserRouter basename="/"` is in `src/main.jsx`.
- DIRECTION §IA reconciliation: the **CustomsIQ pipeline** showpiece lives on
  **Home (`#pipeline`)**, the **9-industry skyline** on **Work (`#industries`)**
  — matching S01's supersession note. Selector NAMES (`hero`/`pipeline`/
  `skyline`) are the locked mount-point names from the Mount-point table.
- `#newsletter` lives in `StubFooter` (global) and resolves from every route;
  the Home `#cta` button + CTA banner target it via `href="#newsletter"`.
- Stubs are data-complete but **motion-free** (no Lenis/ScrollTrigger yet — S03).
  `RootLayout`'s `<Outlet/>` wrapper is a PLAIN block, never a transformed
  scroll container (scar #2). The page-transition seam is S05/S13.
- Chrome (`StubNav`/`StubFooter`) is intentionally MINIMAL — S05 replaces both
  with the real Navbar/Footer; the `#newsletter` anchor + Sender mount
  (`data-sender-form-id="axkAXn"`) must survive that swap.
  <!-- appended by sprint 05 --> **DONE:** swapped to
  `src/components/chrome/Navbar.jsx` / `Footer.jsx` (stubs deleted); the
  anchor + Sender mount survived (see "Global chrome contract (S05)" below).
  `RootLayout` now also mounts `CustomCursor` + the no-op transition seam.

> If DIRECTION chooses a different route count/names, S02 rewrites this whole
> table and the orchestrator re-points S06–S09 from it. `#newsletter` must live
> on exactly one route and be reachable cross-route from the CTA (see
> Integration contracts).

### `src/data/*` single-source modules (S02 — import from here; never hardcode)
<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->

All content mirrors `plans/00-facts/facts.json` (the committed `src/data/*`
files are the single source; if facts.json changes, hand-sync the affected
module). Every component imports the NAMED export below; no component hand-types
counts/ids/links. Export names (consumed by S03–S16):

| module | named export(s) | shape / count |
|---|---|---|
| `src/data/nav.js` | `NAV` | 4 route links `{to,label}` (`/ /work /studio /contact`) |
| `src/data/links.js` | `LINKS` | `{calendly, email, linkedinCompany, emailjs:{service,template,publicKey,fields[],successText}, sender:{formId,account}}` — Calendly lives here ONCE |
| `src/data/team.js` | `TEAM`, `TEAM_CATEGORIES` | 18 `{name,category,specialty,slug,linkedin}`; 18 `linkedin.com/in/` urls |
| `src/data/industries.js` | `INDUSTRIES` | 9 `{industry,stat,subtitle,before,result,after}` |
| `src/data/caseStudies.js` | `CASE_STUDIES` | 2 `{title,description,agents}` |
| `src/data/services.js` | `SERVICES` | 3 `{title,description}` |
| `src/data/process.js` | `PROCESS` | 4 `{num,title,description}` |
| `src/data/testimonials.js` | `TESTIMONIALS` | 3 `{quote,role}` |
| `src/data/logoIndustries.js` | `LOGO_INDUSTRIES` | 8 strings |
| `src/data/hero.js` | `HERO`, (re-exports `CTA_BANNER`) | `{tagline,headline[],subtitle,trustBar[]}` |
| `src/data/ctaBanner.js` | `CTA_BANNER` | `{headline,subtitle,button,badges[]}` (canonical home) |
| `src/data/pipeline.js` | `PIPELINE`, `PIPELINE_STAGES`, `PIPELINE_NUMBERS` | 5 stages + keyNumbers + `{graph:{nodes,edges}, steps[]}` |
| `src/data/brand.js` | `BRAND` | `{name,wordmark,domain}` |
| `src/data/seo.js` | `SEO`, `BASE_SEO` | per-route `{title,description,canonical,ogImage[,keywords]}`; key by route path |

### Head manager API (S02)
<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
- `useDocumentHead({title, description, canonical, ogImage})` —
  `src/lib/useDocumentHead.js`. No new dep (direct DOM upsert in an effect).
  Each page calls `useDocumentHead(SEO[path])`. `index.html` carries the static
  base/Home meta + the Sender script + the SPA-restore snippet (those stay).
- SPA fallback: `public/404.html` (rafgraph single-redirect, `pathSegmentsToKeep=0`)
  + the restore `<script>` in `index.html` `<head>` (runs before the module).

### QA route contract (S02)
<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
- `scripts/qa/routes.mjs` `ROUTES` = the 4 real routes, each with
  `{path,name,ownedIds,showpiece,mobileStatic,legacy:false,expect}`.
  `showpiece` is `null` on every route NOW (stubs carry zero GL; the mount
  boxes are empty placeholders, no `window.__gl`) → verify asserts "no GL chunks
  fetched" on all four. S10/S11/S12 repoint `showpiece` to the real GL selectors.
- `route.expect` makes the facts contract route-aware:
  `{calendlyMin, team, contactForm, senderMount, companyLinked, mailto}` — the 18
  team links gate only on `/studio`, the EmailJS form only on `/contact`;
  Calendly ≥3 + footer integrations gate on every route. `facts_check.mjs` still
  asserts the FULL contract site-wide (21 checks).

---

## Design tokens pointer
<!-- appended by sprint 01 -->

- Source of truth after S01: **`plans/design/TOKENS.md`** (colors, type scale,
  spacing, radii, motion easings/durations) and **`plans/design/DIRECTION.md`**.
- Implementation home (S02+): `tailwind.config.*` + a CSS-vars block in the
  global stylesheet. S01 fills the pointer here; do not duplicate token *values*
  in this file — point at TOKENS.md.

<!-- appended by sprint 01 (final) -->
- **Winner direction:** Route A — "Blueprint → Built" (`plans/design/JUDGE.md`).
- **Token files (final):** `plans/design/TOKENS.md` (machine source — Tailwind
  keys, CSS vars, type scale, easing/duration constants) +
  `plans/design/DIRECTION.md` (binding art direction: Tokens/IA/Motion
  Language/Showpieces/Anti-Wander Rules).
- **CSS-var prefix:** `--k-` (e.g. `--k-ground`, `--k-ink`, `--k-accent`).
- **Tailwind color keys:** `ground / surface / surface-2 / line / line-2 / ink /
  ink-dim / muted / muted-2 / accent / accent-strong` (values only in TOKENS.md).
- **Fonts:** Clash Display 600/700 · Satoshi var · JetBrains Mono var —
  4 self-hosted WOFF2 files in `public/fonts/`, ≤ 220 KB total.
- **Easing constants:** `EASE.PRIMARY = cubic-bezier(0.7, 0, 0.2, 1)`
  (CustomEase `"kPrimary"`), `EASE.SETTLE = "expo.out"`,
  `EASE.SNAP = "back.out(1.4)"` — implemented in `src/lib/motion.js` (S03/S04).
- **Routes (locked):** `/`, `/work`, `/studio`, `/contact`.
- **Showpiece selectors (locked names):** `[data-showpiece="hero"]` (Home H1 —
  DECIDED: CSS/SVG only, no WebGL), `[data-showpiece="pipeline"]` (CustomsIQ
  assemble/disassemble — per DIRECTION.md IA this lives on **Home H2**),
  `[data-showpiece="skyline"]` (9-industry extrude on `/work` W2).

---

## Motion primitives API
<!-- appended by sprint 04 -->

`src/components/motion/*` — the reusable primitives every page sprint composes.
S04 fills the table: one row per primitive with `import path`, `props`, the gate
it applies (reduced-motion/mobile → static final state), and a one-line
behavior. Page sprints compose ONLY these — no ad-hoc GSAP in sections except
via the route-scroll-context.

| primitive | import path | props | gate | behavior |

## Scroll/motion backbone API (S03)
<!-- appended by sprint 03 -->

The backbone every later sprint composes on. Files:
`src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx` · `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js` ·
`src/lib/motion.js` · `src/components/Preloader.jsx`.

### `useRouteScrollContext()` — `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js`
```js
const { scopeRef, add, refreshSoon } = useRouteScrollContext();
```
- **EVERY page component (S06–S13) MUST call this** and register ALL its
  scroll work through it — no ad-hoc ScrollTriggers outside a route context.
- `scopeRef` → attach to the page's root element (plain block, never
  transformed — scar #2). All 5 current pages already do this.
- `add(fn)` → run `fn` inside the route's `gsap.context`. Safe to call from
  section effects that run BEFORE the page's context exists (child effects run
  first): callbacks queue and flush into the context at creation. Sections that
  unmount independently of the route must manage their own nested
  `gsap.context` + revert.
- On route unmount the context `revert()`s — every trigger/tween dies BEFORE
  the next route's effects run (scar #9, proven by the verify.mjs 3×-cycle
  assertion).
- The hook auto-creates one zero-cost **route-sentinel** ScrollTrigger
  (id `route-sentinel`) per route — that is why `__stCount()` baseline is 1,
  not 0. Do not remove it; the leak assertion measures against it.
- **Scar #1 reminder** (in the hook's JSDoc): never `self.kill()` inside
  `onUpdate`/`onToggle` — `gsap.delayedCall(0, () => self.kill())`.

### `refreshSoon(delaySec = 0.15)` — same module, also exported standalone
- THE one coordinated, debounced `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` channel (scar #3).
  Calls coalesce. Already wired: `document.fonts.ready` + `preloaderDone` →
  refresh; every route mount → refresh; nav scroll-reset → refresh.
- **S13 calls `refreshSoon()` after its transition-in completes.** Sections
  NEVER call `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` directly.

### SmoothScroll provider — `src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx`
- Mounted ONCE in `src/main.jsx` (inside `BrowserRouter`, outside the Outlet).
  Lenis scrolls the REAL window, driven from `gsap.ticker`
  (`lagSmoothing(0)`); survives navigation; on route change does
  `lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })` + `refreshSoon()`.
- `useLenis()` context hook → the live instance (null on native tiers/early).
- Reduced-motion OR mobile (≤768) ⇒ **no Lenis at all** (native scroll tier).

### Preloader — `src/components/Preloader.jsx` (mounted in `RootLayout`)
- `[data-preloader]` veil, z-[100]; "All Is. Now" assemble + 0→100 counter on
  the drafting grid; pure CSS/GSAP, no deps. Total ≈2.4s.
- Once per session: sessionStorage key **`k-preloaded`** (set on completion
  AND on every skip path).
- Skip paths (render null, no veil): session flag · `?skippreload` ·
  `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- **`preloaderDone`** — `window` CustomEvent dispatched on EVERY path
  (completed or skipped) + `window.__preloaderDone = true`. The refresh
  coordinator and `?jump` wait on it. S13's transition system must NOT gate on
  the preloader existing — gate on the event/flag.
- Scroll-lock while shown: `documentElement.style.overflow = "hidden"` +
  `lenis.stop()`; both restored on exit.

### Motion constants — `src/lib/motion.js` (TOKENS §5, live)
- `EASE.PRIMARY` = CustomEase **"kPrimary"** (`cubic-bezier(0.7,0,0.2,1)`,
  registered at module load) · `EASE.SETTLE` = `"expo.out"` · `EASE.SNAP` =
  `"back.out(1.4)"` (never on opacity). CSS twin: `EASE_CSS_PRIMARY`.
- `DUR.XS/SM/MD/LG/SCENE` = 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7/1.0s · `STAGGER.LINE/CELL` =
  0.06/0.04. One easing language — import from here only (motion law 1).
- Legacy exports (`EASE_OUT`, `revealVariants`, …) remain for pre-rebuild
  components only; they die with the old components (S05+).

(S07/S12); testimonials are a HOME act (H5).** Sections live in
`src/sections/home/*` composed by `src/pages/Home.jsx` (scopeRef on the page
root; primitives own their own contexts).

- **`[data-showpiece="hero"]`** (`HomeHero.jsx`) — `relative`, aspect-square,
  max-w 480px. Child `[data-hero-poster]` = the static SVG polyhedron (top
  faces accent-inked, lower scaffolding dashed ghost). **S10:** animate/replace
  INSIDE the mount; the poster is the reduced/none design — keep it as the
  static tier. CSS/SVG only (DECIDED — no WebGL here).
- **`[data-showpiece="pipeline"]`** (`PipelineShell.jsx`) — `relative`, tall and

  `useRouteScrollContext().add()` (scar #9) with a scar-#5 latch, and reuse the

  mounts relative+sized, fallback content (5 stages + key numbers), 8/3/3
  facts, hero CTAs, CTA-banner→`#newsletter` click reach, reduced/mobile
  static tiers (zero pins).
- **SplitLines fix (S04 primitive, applied S06):** its async `fonts.ready`/
  resize `setup()` now registers through `ctx.add()` — previously the tween +
  trigger ESCAPED the gsap.context and duplicated under StrictMode on every
  SPA re-mount (scar #7). Do not move setup() back outside the context.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# CONTRACTS — the living technical contract

The binding interface between sprints. It grows: most sections below are
skeletons now; the named sprint **appends** to them at runtime. When you add a
selector, id, data-attr, global, or API, you MUST record it here in the same
commit, so QA assertions and later sprints stay in sync.

**Authority:** `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > DIRECTION/TOKENS > sprint file`. This doc
beats DIRECTION and the sprint file on any **locked id / selector / integration
id**. It does not override PROTOCOL.

**How to append:** find your sprint's section, replace its `(empty — …)`
placeholder or add rows beneath the existing ones. Keep the
`<!-- appended by sprint NN -->` marker so the next author knows where their
turn begins. Never delete a prior sprint's entries; if you supersede one, strike
it and add the replacement with a note.

---

## Route map
<!-- appended by sprint 02 (then refined by later page sprints 06-09) -->

The router IA. S02 fills the real rows from `design/DIRECTION.md`'s IA table.
Page sprints (S06–S09) fill in their owned section ids. QA's
`scripts/qa/routes.mjs` mirrors this table — keep them in sync.

| path | component (src path) | owned section ids / anchors | showpiece mount(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `src/pages/Home.jsx` | `#hero #pipeline #industries #services #testimonials #cta` | `data-showpiece="hero"` (CSS/SVG, S10) + `data-showpiece="pipeline"` (WebGL, S11) |
| `/work` | `src/pages/Work.jsx` | `#case-studies #industries #recap` | `data-showpiece="skyline"` (WebGL, S11/S12) |
| `/studio` | `src/pages/Studio.jsx` | `#process #team #manifesto` | — (zero-GL route) |
| `/contact` | `src/pages/Contact.jsx` | `#contact` | — (zero-GL route) |

<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
**S02 router map notes (rewrote the skeleton per DIRECTION §IA):**
- Router: `src/App.jsx` = `<Routes>` with a layout route (`RootLayout.jsx` →
  `StubNav` + `<Outlet/>` + `StubFooter`); catch-all `*` → `NotFound.jsx`
  (unique id `#not-found`). `BrowserRouter basename="/"` is in `src/main.jsx`.
- DIRECTION §IA reconciliation: the **CustomsIQ pipeline** showpiece lives on
  **Home (`#pipeline`)**, the **9-industry skyline** on **Work (`#industries`)**
  — matching S01's supersession note. Selector NAMES (`hero`/`pipeline`/
  `skyline`) are the locked mount-point names from the Mount-point table.
- `#newsletter` lives in `StubFooter` (global) and resolves from every route;
  the Home `#cta` button + CTA banner target it via `href="#newsletter"`.
- Stubs are data-complete but **motion-free** (no Lenis/ScrollTrigger yet — S03).
  `RootLayout`'s `<Outlet/>` wrapper is a PLAIN block, never a transformed
  scroll container (scar #2). The page-transition seam is S05/S13.
- Chrome (`StubNav`/`StubFooter`) is intentionally MINIMAL — S05 replaces both
  with the real Navbar/Footer; the `#newsletter` anchor + Sender mount
  (`data-sender-form-id="axkAXn"`) must survive that swap.
  <!-- appended by sprint 05 --> **DONE:** swapped to
  `src/components/chrome/Navbar.jsx` / `Footer.jsx` (stubs deleted); the
  anchor + Sender mount survived (see "Global chrome contract (S05)" below).
  `RootLayout` now also mounts `CustomCursor` + the no-op transition seam.

> If DIRECTION chooses a different route count/names, S02 rewrites this whole
> table and the orchestrator re-points S06–S09 from it. `#newsletter` must live
> on exactly one route and be reachable cross-route from the CTA (see
> Integration contracts).

### `src/data/*` single-source modules (S02 — import from here; never hardcode)
<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->

All content mirrors `plans/00-facts/facts.json` (the committed `src/data/*`
files are the single source; if facts.json changes, hand-sync the affected
module). Every component imports the NAMED export below; no component hand-types
counts/ids/links. Export names (consumed by S03–S16):

| module | named export(s) | shape / count |
|---|---|---|
| `src/data/nav.js` | `NAV` | 4 route links `{to,label}` (`/ /work /studio /contact`) |
| `src/data/links.js` | `LINKS` | `{calendly, email, linkedinCompany, emailjs:{service,template,publicKey,fields[],successText}, sender:{formId,account}}` — Calendly lives here ONCE |
| `src/data/team.js` | `TEAM`, `TEAM_CATEGORIES` | 18 `{name,category,specialty,slug,linkedin}`; 18 `linkedin.com/in/` urls |
| `src/data/industries.js` | `INDUSTRIES` | 9 `{industry,stat,subtitle,before,result,after}` |
| `src/data/caseStudies.js` | `CASE_STUDIES` | 2 `{title,description,agents}` |
| `src/data/services.js` | `SERVICES` | 3 `{title,description}` |
| `src/data/process.js` | `PROCESS` | 4 `{num,title,description}` |
| `src/data/testimonials.js` | `TESTIMONIALS` | 3 `{quote,role}` |
| `src/data/logoIndustries.js` | `LOGO_INDUSTRIES` | 8 strings |
| `src/data/hero.js` | `HERO`, (re-exports `CTA_BANNER`) | `{tagline,headline[],subtitle,trustBar[]}` |
| `src/data/ctaBanner.js` | `CTA_BANNER` | `{headline,subtitle,button,badges[]}` (canonical home) |
| `src/data/pipeline.js` | `PIPELINE`, `PIPELINE_STAGES`, `PIPELINE_NUMBERS` | 5 stages + keyNumbers + `{graph:{nodes,edges}, steps[]}` |
| `src/data/brand.js` | `BRAND` | `{name,wordmark,domain}` |
| `src/data/seo.js` | `SEO`, `BASE_SEO` | per-route `{title,description,canonical,ogImage[,keywords]}`; key by route path |

### Head manager API (S02)
<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
- `useDocumentHead({title, description, canonical, ogImage})` —
  `src/lib/useDocumentHead.js`. No new dep (direct DOM upsert in an effect).
  Each page calls `useDocumentHead(SEO[path])`. `index.html` carries the static
  base/Home meta + the Sender script + the SPA-restore snippet (those stay).
- SPA fallback: `public/404.html` (rafgraph single-redirect, `pathSegmentsToKeep=0`)
  + the restore `<script>` in `index.html` `<head>` (runs before the module).

### QA route contract (S02)
<!-- appended by sprint 02 -->
- `scripts/qa/routes.mjs` `ROUTES` = the 4 real routes, each with
  `{path,name,ownedIds,showpiece,mobileStatic,legacy:false,expect}`.
  `showpiece` is `null` on every route NOW (stubs carry zero GL; the mount
  boxes are empty placeholders, no `window.__gl`) → verify asserts "no GL chunks
  fetched" on all four. S10/S11/S12 repoint `showpiece` to the real GL selectors.
- `route.expect` makes the facts contract route-aware:
  `{calendlyMin, team, contactForm, senderMount, companyLinked, mailto}` — the 18
  team links gate only on `/studio`, the EmailJS form only on `/contact`;
  Calendly ≥3 + footer integrations gate on every route. `facts_check.mjs` still
  asserts the FULL contract site-wide (21 checks).

---

## Design tokens pointer
<!-- appended by sprint 01 -->

- Source of truth after S01: **`plans/design/TOKENS.md`** (colors, type scale,
  spacing, radii, motion easings/durations) and **`plans/design/DIRECTION.md`**.
- Implementation home (S02+): `tailwind.config.*` + a CSS-vars block in the
  global stylesheet. S01 fills the pointer here; do not duplicate token *values*
  in this file — point at TOKENS.md.

<!-- appended by sprint 01 (final) -->
- **Winner direction:** Route A — "Blueprint → Built" (`plans/design/JUDGE.md`).
- **Token files (final):** `plans/design/TOKENS.md` (machine source — Tailwind
  keys, CSS vars, type scale, easing/duration constants) +
  `plans/design/DIRECTION.md` (binding art direction: Tokens/IA/Motion
  Language/Showpieces/Anti-Wander Rules).
- **CSS-var prefix:** `--k-` (e.g. `--k-ground`, `--k-ink`, `--k-accent`).
- **Tailwind color keys:** `ground / surface / surface-2 / line / line-2 / ink /
  ink-dim / muted / muted-2 / accent / accent-strong` (values only in TOKENS.md).
- **Fonts:** Clash Display 600/700 · Satoshi var · JetBrains Mono var —
  4 self-hosted WOFF2 files in `public/fonts/`, ≤ 220 KB total.
- **Easing constants:** `EASE.PRIMARY = cubic-bezier(0.7, 0, 0.2, 1)`
  (CustomEase `"kPrimary"`), `EASE.SETTLE = "expo.out"`,
  `EASE.SNAP = "back.out(1.4)"` — implemented in `src/lib/motion.js` (S03/S04).
- **Routes (locked):** `/`, `/work`, `/studio`, `/contact`.
- **Showpiece selectors (locked names):** `[data-showpiece="hero"]` (Home H1 —
  DECIDED: CSS/SVG only, no WebGL), `[data-showpiece="pipeline"]` (CustomsIQ
  assemble/disassemble — per DIRECTION.md IA this lives on **Home H2**),
  `[data-showpiece="skyline"]` (9-industry extrude on `/work` W2).

---

## Motion primitives API
<!-- appended by sprint 04 -->

`src/components/motion/*` — the reusable primitives every page sprint composes.
S04 fills the table: one row per primitive with `import path`, `props`, the gate
it applies (reduced-motion/mobile → static final state), and a one-line
behavior. Page sprints compose ONLY these — no ad-hoc GSAP in sections except
via the route-scroll-context.

| primitive | import path | props | gate | behavior |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `SplitLines` (aliases `MaskText`, `SplitTextReveal`) | `src/components/motion/SplitLines.jsx` | `as="h2", children:string, mode="lines"\|"words", delay, stagger, className` | reduced/mobile → plain string, fully visible | waits `document.fonts.ready` (scar #3), splits words → groups lines by offsetTop → masked yPercent reveal, once, holds. `data-split="done"` on complete |
| `InkReveal` | `src/components/motion/InkReveal.jsx` | `as, from="up"\|"left"\|"right"\|"none", delay, duration, start, className, children` | reduced/mobile → final state | ghost (opacity 0 + TRAVEL px + clip inset) → inked strike, EASE.PRIMARY, once, holds forever. Root `relative` (scar #4), `data-revealed="true"` on complete |
| `DraftLine` | `src/components/motion/DraftLine.jsx` | `orient="h"\|"v"\|"diag", accent, thickness, scrub, className` | reduced/mobile → fully drawn | SVG stroke-dashoffset draw, once or scrubbed-with-latch (scar #5); strokes `currentColor` from token text classes |
| `DimensionMark` | `src/components/motion/DimensionMark.jsx` | `label, side="left"\|"top", accent, className` | reduced/mobile → drawn + visible | mono micro-label + ~48px leader line draws then label inks in, once |
| `SnapStack` (alias `CardStack`) | `src/components/motion/SnapStack.jsx` | `stagger, start, className, children` (direct children = cards; caller owns grid layout) | reduced/mobile → plain stacked cards | cards arrive from right + alternating rotation, EASE.SNAP on transforms ONLY, EASE.PRIMARY on opacity, once, holds |
| `AxisRail` (alias `HorizontalPin`) | `src/components/motion/AxisRail.jsx` | `markers, className, children` (each child = one panel) | reduced/mobile → vertical stack, NO pin | pins root `+=(n-1)*100%`, scrub 0.6, track xPercent lateral travel + accent progress rail + mono counter; scar-#5 latch sets `data-latched="end"\|"start"` |
| `KineticMarquee` | `src/components/motion/KineticMarquee.jsx` | `items:string[], speed=40, velocityFactor, skewOnVelocity=false (ONLY the industries band may set true), className, renderItem` | reduced/mobile → static flex-wrap list | duplicated row driven by ONE gsap.ticker reading `scrollBus.velocity` (the one shared velocity — see `src/lib/scrollBus.js`); pauses offscreen via onToggle flag |
| `MagneticButton` | `src/components/motion/MagneticButton.jsx` | `as="button"\|"a", strength=0.35, className, children, ...rest` | coarse pointer OR reduced/mobile → plain tap target | quickTo magnet toward cursor (root ×strength, label ×strength/2), rect cached per pointerenter, settles on leave |
| `MagneticCursor` | `src/components/motion/MagneticCursor.jsx` | `size=32` | coarse/reduced/mobile → renders `null` (native cursor untouched) | fixed dot+ring lerped via one gsap.ticker + quickSetters; ring scales over `a, button, [data-cursor]`. Ring carries `data-magnetic-cursor` |
| `CountStrike` (supersedes legacy `CountUp.jsx`) | `src/components/motion/CountStrike.jsx` | `to, from=0, decimals, prefix, suffix, duration=DUR.SCENE, format?, className, as="span"` | reduced/mobile → final number immediately | DOM ships the FINAL formatted value (en-IN grouping, `tabular-nums`); motion tier rewinds + counts up once at center, writes textContent (no setState), `data-struck="true"` |
| `ScrubScene` | `src/components/motion/ScrubScene.jsx` | `length="+=200%", pin=true, scrub=0.6, onProgress(p), latch=true, cssVar=false, className, children` | reduced/mobile → NO pin, children plain, `onProgress(1)` once, `data-state="final"` | **THE generic showpiece harness (S10/S11 build on it).** Pins root, scrubs, calls `onProgress(self.progress)` imperatively each update — consumers must NEVER setState per frame and must defer any kill via `gsap.delayedCall` (scar #1). Scar-#5 latch commits 1/0 on leave/leaveBack |

## Scroll/motion backbone API (S03)
<!-- appended by sprint 03 -->

The backbone every later sprint composes on. Files:
`src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx` · `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js` ·
`src/lib/motion.js` · `src/components/Preloader.jsx`.

### `useRouteScrollContext()` — `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js`
```js
const { scopeRef, add, refreshSoon } = useRouteScrollContext();
```
- **EVERY page component (S06–S13) MUST call this** and register ALL its
  scroll work through it — no ad-hoc ScrollTriggers outside a route context.
- `scopeRef` → attach to the page's root element (plain block, never
  transformed — scar #2). All 5 current pages already do this.
- `add(fn)` → run `fn` inside the route's `gsap.context`. Safe to call from
  section effects that run BEFORE the page's context exists (child effects run
  first): callbacks queue and flush into the context at creation. Sections that
  unmount independently of the route must manage their own nested
  `gsap.context` + revert.
- On route unmount the context `revert()`s — every trigger/tween dies BEFORE
  the next route's effects run (scar #9, proven by the verify.mjs 3×-cycle
  assertion).
- The hook auto-creates one zero-cost **route-sentinel** ScrollTrigger
  (id `route-sentinel`) per route — that is why `__stCount()` baseline is 1,
  not 0. Do not remove it; the leak assertion measures against it.
- **Scar #1 reminder** (in the hook's JSDoc): never `self.kill()` inside
  `onUpdate`/`onToggle` — `gsap.delayedCall(0, () => self.kill())`.

### `refreshSoon(delaySec = 0.15)` — same module, also exported standalone
- THE one coordinated, debounced `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` channel (scar #3).
  Calls coalesce. Already wired: `document.fonts.ready` + `preloaderDone` →
  refresh; every route mount → refresh; nav scroll-reset → refresh.
- **S13 calls `refreshSoon()` after its transition-in completes.** Sections
  NEVER call `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` directly.

### SmoothScroll provider — `src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx`
- Mounted ONCE in `src/main.jsx` (inside `BrowserRouter`, outside the Outlet).
  Lenis scrolls the REAL window, driven from `gsap.ticker`
  (`lagSmoothing(0)`); survives navigation; on route change does
  `lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })` + `refreshSoon()`.
- `useLenis()` context hook → the live instance (null on native tiers/early).
- Reduced-motion OR mobile (≤768) ⇒ **no Lenis at all** (native scroll tier).

### Preloader — `src/components/Preloader.jsx` (mounted in `RootLayout`)
- `[data-preloader]` veil, z-[100]; "All Is. Now" assemble + 0→100 counter on
  the drafting grid; pure CSS/GSAP, no deps. Total ≈2.4s.
- Once per session: sessionStorage key **`k-preloaded`** (set on completion
  AND on every skip path).
- Skip paths (render null, no veil): session flag · `?skippreload` ·
  `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- **`preloaderDone`** — `window` CustomEvent dispatched on EVERY path
  (completed or skipped) + `window.__preloaderDone = true`. The refresh
  coordinator and `?jump` wait on it. S13's transition system must NOT gate on
  the preloader existing — gate on the event/flag.
- Scroll-lock while shown: `documentElement.style.overflow = "hidden"` +
  `lenis.stop()`; both restored on exit.

### Motion constants — `src/lib/motion.js` (TOKENS §5, live)
- `EASE.PRIMARY` = CustomEase **"kPrimary"** (`cubic-bezier(0.7,0,0.2,1)`,
  registered at module load) · `EASE.SETTLE` = `"expo.out"` · `EASE.SNAP` =
  `"back.out(1.4)"` (never on opacity). CSS twin: `EASE_CSS_PRIMARY`.
- `DUR.XS/SM/MD/LG/SCENE` = 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7/1.0s · `STAGGER.LINE/CELL` =
  0.06/0.04. One easing language — import from here only (motion law 1).
- Legacy exports (`EASE_OUT`, `revealVariants`, …) remain for pre-rebuild
  components only; they die with the old components (S05+).

# CONTRACTS — the living technical contract

The binding interface between sprints. It grows: most sections below are
skeletons now; the named sprint **appends** to them at runtime. When you add a
selector, id, data-attr, global, or API, you MUST record it here in the same
commit, so QA assertions and later sprints stay in sync.

**Authority:** `PROTOCOL > CONTRACTS > DIRECTION/TOKENS > sprint file`. This doc
beats DIRECTION and the sprint file on any **locked id / selector / integration
id**. It does not override PROTOCOL.

**How to append:** find your sprint's section, replace its `(empty — …)`
placeholder or add rows beneath the existing ones. Keep the
`<!-- appended by sprint NN -->` marker so the next author knows where their
turn begins. Never delete a prior sprint's entries; if you supersede one, strike
it and add the replacement with a note.

## Scroll/motion backbone API (S03)
<!-- appended by sprint 03 -->

The backbone every later sprint composes on. Files:
`src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx` · `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js` ·
`src/lib/motion.js` · `src/components/Preloader.jsx`.

### `useRouteScrollContext()` — `src/lib/useRouteScrollContext.js`
```js
const { scopeRef, add, refreshSoon } = useRouteScrollContext();
```
- **EVERY page component (S06–S13) MUST call this** and register ALL its
  scroll work through it — no ad-hoc ScrollTriggers outside a route context.
- `scopeRef` → attach to the page's root element (plain block, never
  transformed — scar #2). All 5 current pages already do this.
- `add(fn)` → run `fn` inside the route's `gsap.context`. Safe to call from
  section effects that run BEFORE the page's context exists (child effects run
  first): callbacks queue and flush into the context at creation. Sections that
  unmount independently of the route must manage their own nested
  `gsap.context` + revert.
- On route unmount the context `revert()`s — every trigger/tween dies BEFORE
  the next route's effects run (scar #9, proven by the verify.mjs 3×-cycle
  assertion).
- The hook auto-creates one zero-cost **route-sentinel** ScrollTrigger
  (id `route-sentinel`) per route — that is why `__stCount()` baseline is 1,
  not 0. Do not remove it; the leak assertion measures against it.
- **Scar #1 reminder** (in the hook's JSDoc): never `self.kill()` inside
  `onUpdate`/`onToggle` — `gsap.delayedCall(0, () => self.kill())`.

### `refreshSoon(delaySec = 0.15)` — same module, also exported standalone
- THE one coordinated, debounced `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` channel (scar #3).
  Calls coalesce. Already wired: `document.fonts.ready` + `preloaderDone` →
  refresh; every route mount → refresh; nav scroll-reset → refresh.
- **S13 calls `refreshSoon()` after its transition-in completes.** Sections
  NEVER call `ScrollTrigger.refresh()` directly.

### SmoothScroll provider — `src/lib/SmoothScroll.jsx`
- Mounted ONCE in `src/main.jsx` (inside `BrowserRouter`, outside the Outlet).
  Lenis scrolls the REAL window, driven from `gsap.ticker`
  (`lagSmoothing(0)`); survives navigation; on route change does
  `lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true })` + `refreshSoon()`.
- `useLenis()` context hook → the live instance (null on native tiers/early).
- Reduced-motion OR mobile (≤768) ⇒ **no Lenis at all** (native scroll tier).

### Preloader — `src/components/Preloader.jsx` (mounted in `RootLayout`)
- `[data-preloader]` veil, z-[100]; "All Is. Now" assemble + 0→100 counter on
  the drafting grid; pure CSS/GSAP, no deps. Total ≈2.4s.
- Once per session: sessionStorage key **`k-preloaded`** (set on completion
  AND on every skip path).
- Skip paths (render null, no veil): session flag · `?skippreload` ·
  `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- **`preloaderDone`** — `window` CustomEvent dispatched on EVERY path
  (completed or skipped) + `window.__preloaderDone = true`. The refresh
  coordinator and `?jump` wait on it. S13's transition system must NOT gate on
  the preloader existing — gate on the event/flag.
- Scroll-lock while shown: `documentElement.style.overflow = "hidden"` +
  `lenis.stop()`; both restored on exit.

### Motion constants — `src/lib/motion.js` (TOKENS §5, live)
- `EASE.PRIMARY` = CustomEase **"kPrimary"** (`cubic-bezier(0.7,0,0.2,1)`,
  registered at module load) · `EASE.SETTLE` = `"expo.out"` · `EASE.SNAP` =
  `"back.out(1.4)"` (never on opacity). CSS twin: `EASE_CSS_PRIMARY`.
- `DUR.XS/SM/MD/LG/SCENE` = 0.3/0.5/0.6/0.7/1.0s · `STAGGER.LINE/CELL` =
  0.06/0.04. One easing language — import from here only (motion law 1).
- Legacy exports (`EASE_OUT`, `revealVariants`, …) remain for pre-rebuild
  components only; they die with the old components (S05+).
